name: Brisket with Sweet Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds sweet potatoes, peeled; cut into half-slices
1.5 pounds parsnips, peeled; cut into chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in the pressure cooker.
2. Brown the meat well on both sides over high heat, lifting it frequently with tongs or a spatula to prevent sticking. Add additional fat if needed. Set aside.
3. In the remaining oil, sauté celery, carrots, and onion for 3 minutes, stirring occasionally and scraping up any browned bits of meat or vegetable from the bottom of the pot.
4. Add beef stock, water, cinnamon, nutmeg, and allspice to the cooker.
5. Place the rack into the mixture, positioning the meat on it with the fat side facing up. Lock the lid in place over high heat and bring to high pressure.
6. Maintain high pressure for 55 minutes.
7. Allow the pressure to drop naturally for about 10-15 minutes. Remove the lid, tilting it away from you to let excess steam escape.
8. If the brisket is tender enough to be easily pried with a fork, serve immediately. Otherwise, lock the lid back in place and return to high pressure for another 10-15 minutes.
9. Stir in cider until approximately 2 cups are reached. Add sweet potatoes, parsnips, and prunes. Lock the lid in place and bring to high pressure over high heat. Cook for 3 minutes.
10. Quick-release the pressure. Season with salt and pepper.
11. Slice the brisket diagonally, return it to the cooker, and spoon gravy and vegetables on top. Cover but do not lock. Cook over medium heat until the meat is warmed through.
12. Arrange on a platter and serve with the vegetables and gravy poured on top.
